Dimocarpus longan, also known as the longan tree, is a plant species in the family Sapindaceae. The longan tree is a tropical evergreen tree native to Asia, with a rich history of traditional use in medicine and nutrition.

The health benefits of Dimocarpus longan include antioxidant, anti-inflammatory, and antimicrobial properties, which can aid in reducing oxidative stress, inflammation, and the risk of certain diseases such as cancer and cardiovascular disease. The longan fruit has also been traditionally used to support eye health, reduce fever, and improve digestion.

The bioactive constituents of Dimocarpus longan include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and saponins, which have been identified as the primary compounds responsible for its medicinal properties. Other bioactive compounds such as vitamin C, potassium, and fiber are also present in the longan fruit.

The most common herbal preparations made with Dimocarpus longan include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support eye health, reduce fever, and improve digestion. Longan fruit is also consumed as a fresh fruit or dried as a snack, and is often used in traditional Chinese medicine to support overall health and well-being.
